results.name: Kylo Ren's lightsaber from Star Wars
results.description:
High-quality 3D model of Kylo Ren's iconic lightsaber. This model features a unique "unstable" look created with high-poly sculpting, giving the blade its signature aggressive energy.

**Main Features:**
Ready for Render: Pre-configured materials for Eevee and Cycles (Bloom effect included).
Clean Geometry: One single mesh with applied Scale (1.000).
Sculpted Details: Hand-sculpted blade for a realistic "crackling energy" effect.
Game Ready: Low-poly handle combined with a high-impact blade look.

**Technical Specs:**
Formats: .blend (native), .fbx, .obj.
Scale: 1:1 real-world scale.
Compatibility: Works perfectly with Blender, Unity, and Unreal Engine.

results.name: ARC-170 fighter
results.description: This is low-poly model of Aggressive Reconnaissance-170 (ARC-170) space superiority fighter from Star Wars Episode III. This model was originally created by minilogoguy18 and Dark_Cuillere with some remastering done by myself recently

results.name: Sands of Journey
results.description:
In this little world inspired by Journey, Sable the game and Star Wars, the small orange beings were left behind with the hope of a purpose greater than all the riches and technologies of the earth.

Through low-poly modeling and simplistic textures, I wanted to communicate the richness of the earth and the great architectural achievements in contrast to the simple clothes of the orange beings and mounds of unpolished stones on the ground.

By looking at inspirations from other works on the internet and seeing topologies here and there, I'm trying to improve my understanding of topology and how it can relate to bone structures for animation using blender 3D software

results.name: Republic Gunship (LAAT)
results.description: This is low-poly model of Republic Gunship (LAAT - Low Altitude Assault Transport) used by the Republic during the clone wars. This model was originally created by Mars Marshall (NeoMarz1) for a Star Wars game with some remastering done by myself later on.

results.name: At-at  Star wars
results.description: the low poly model of AT-AT (All Terrain Armored Transport) — The Galactic Empire's iconic 22.5-meter-long, four-legged walker from Star Wars

results.name: Dark saber V1 low poly
results.description: The Dark saber from star wars. This is my first time creating and publishing somthing using blender 5.0, but i hope to improve the modle in the future. If you go into blender and import this file, you can make it glow by creating a material as emmission. then go to geomatry nodes and add a new one at the top. then when the lines split to group output and viewer, put a glare node and set it to bloom. then go back to layout and next to the renders viewport shading at the top left, click the drop down and select alwayss on compositer. you can also adjust the stranght of the glow in the material settings by sliding strength. If you have any ideas on how i could improve this, plz let me know in the comments!

results.name: Star Wars Monster
results.description: Created as a commissioned project for a studio producing Minecraft mod content for YouTube.

results.name: e5_Blaster
results.description:
This is the E5 Blaster rifle used by the B1 Battle Droids in star wars. This asset is used in the Republic Commando RTX Remix mod.

Model was made in blender for the Low-polym (and some high poly) and the Majority of the High-Poly was created in Plasticity.
The model contains 4 Texture maps at 4096X4096 dimensions (Albedo, Roughness, Metalness, Normal).
~24.3K Tris.

The RTX Remix mod can be viewed here. Stay put for updates where this model will be added to the mod as well as some others by me soon.
https://www.moddb.com/mods/star-wars-republic-commando-rtx1

results.name: Stylized 3D Printable Industrial Chemical Plant
results.description:
Description: A versatile, low-poly stylized Chemical Plant model featuring industrial smokestacks, reinforced silos, and glowing green vats. This asset is specifically designed for multi-purpose use, ranging from 3D printing for tabletop gaming to digital environment dioramas.

The structure is built on a solid foundation base, making it ideal for physical printing without needing complex supports for the main body. Its distinct silhouette and "toxic" green accents make it a perfect fit for sci-fi, post-apocalyptic, or modern industrial settings.

Optimized for 3D Printing: Sturdy geometry suitable for resin or FDM printing.

Design: Stylized industrial aesthetic with glowing windows and chemical containers.

Applications: Perfect for Warhammer 40k terrain, Star Wars Legion, D&D dioramas, or game engine background assets.

Features: Multiple levels, rooftop pipes, and a integrated baseplate for easy placement in scenes.
